NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. God be merciful to us and bless us, And cause His face to shine upon us, Selah
NKJV.2: That Your way may be known on earth,  Your salvation among all nations.
NKJV.3: Let the peoples praise You, O God; Let all the peoples praise You.
NKJV.4: Oh, let the nations be glad and sing for joy! For You shall judge the people righteously, And govern the nations on earth. Selah
NKJV.5: Let the peoples praise You, O God; Let all the peoples praise You.
NKJV.6: Then the earth shall yield her increase; God, our own God, shall bless us.
NKJV.7: God shall bless us, And all the ends of the earth shall fear Him.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> on Neginoth<S>5058</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S>. God<S>430</S> be merciful<S>2603</S> unto us, and bless<S>1288</S> us; and cause his face<S>6440</S> to shine<S>215</S> upon us; Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>upon: Heb. with</sup>
KJV.2: That thy way<S>1870</S> may be known<S>3045</S> upon earth<S>776</S>, thy saving health<S>3444</S> among all nations<S>1471</S>.
KJV.3: Let the people<S>5971</S> praise<S>3034</S> thee, O God<S>430</S>; let all the people<S>5971</S> praise<S>3034</S> thee.
KJV.4: O let the nations<S>3816</S> be glad<S>8055</S> and sing for joy<S>7442</S>: for thou shalt judge<S>8199</S> the people<S>5971</S> righteously<S>4334</S>, and govern<S>5148</S> the nations<S>3816</S> upon earth<S>776</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>govern: Heb. lead</sup>
KJV.5: Let the people<S>5971</S> praise<S>3034</S> thee, O God<S>430</S>; let all the people<S>5971</S> praise<S>3034</S> thee.
KJV.6: Then shall the earth<S>776</S> yield<S>5414</S> her increase<S>2981</S>; and God<S>430</S>, even our own God<S>430</S>, shall bless<S>1288</S> us.
KJV.7: God<S>430</S> shall bless<S>1288</S> us; and all the ends<S>657</S> of the earth<S>776</S> shall fear<S>3372</S> him.

ESV.1: May God be gracious to us and bless us and make his face to shine upon us, Selah
ESV.2: that your way may be known on earth, your saving power among all nations.
ESV.3: Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples praise you!
ESV.4: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you judge the peoples with equity and guide the nations upon earth. Selah
ESV.5: Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples praise you!
ESV.6: The earth has yielded its increase; God, our God, shall bless us.
ESV.7: God shall bless us; let all the ends of the earth fear him!

NASB.1: For the choir director; with stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. God be gracious to us and bless us, [And] cause His face to shine upon us-- Selah.
NASB.2: That Your way may be known on the earth, Your salvation among all nations.
NASB.3: Let the peoples praise You, O God; Let all the peoples praise You.
NASB.4: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy; For You will judge the peoples with uprightness And guide the nations on the earth. Selah.
NASB.5: Let the peoples praise You, O God; Let all the peoples praise You.
NASB.6: The earth has yielded its produce; God, our God, blesses us.
NASB.7: God blesses us, That all the ends of the earth may fear Him.

NLT.1:
May God be merciful and bless us.
May his face smile with favor on us. Interlude
NLT.2:
May your ways be known throughout the earth,
your saving power among people everywhere.
NLT.3:
May the nations praise you, O God.
Yes, may all the nations praise you.
NLT.4:
Let the whole world sing for joy,
because you govern the nations with justice
and guide the people of the whole world. Interlude
NLT.5:
May the nations praise you, O God.
Yes, may all the nations praise you.
NLT.6:
Then the earth will yield its harvests,
and God, our God, will richly bless us.
NLT.7:
Yes, God will bless us,
and people all over the world will fear him.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. A Psalm. A song. May God be merciful to us, bless us, and cause his face to shine on us. Selah.
WEB.2: That your way may be known on earth, and your salvation among all nations,
WEB.3: let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.
WEB.4: Oh let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you will judge the peoples with equity, and govern the nations on earth. Selah.
WEB.5: Let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.
WEB.6: The earth has yielded its increase. God, even our own God, will bless us.
WEB.7: God will bless us. All the ends of the earth shall fear him.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: with stringed instruments. A psalm. A song. [1] May God be gracious to us and bless us;
may he make his face shine upon us ⓒ Selah
CSB17.2:
so that your way may be known on earth,
your salvation among all nations. ⓓ
CSB17.3:
Let the peoples praise you, God;
let all the peoples praise you. ⓔ
CSB17.4:
Let the nations rejoice and shout for joy,
for you judge the peoples with fairness
and lead the nations on earth. ⓕ Selah
CSB17.5:
Let the peoples praise you, God,
let all the peoples praise you. ⓖ
CSB17.6:
The earth has produced its harvest;
God, our God, blesses us. ⓗ
CSB17.7:
God will bless us,
and all the ends of the earth will fear him. ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. God be gracious and kind-hearted to us and bless us,
And make His face shine [with favor] on us— Selah.
AMP.2:
That Your way may be known on earth,
Your salvation and deliverance among all nations.
AMP.3:
Let the peoples praise You, O God;
Let all the peoples praise You.
AMP.4:
Let the nations be glad and sing for joy,
For You will judge the people fairly
And guide the nations on earth. Selah.
AMP.5:
Let the peoples praise You, O God;
Let all the peoples praise You.
AMP.6:
The earth has yielded its harvest [as evidence of His approval];
God, our God, blesses us.
AMP.7:
God blesses us,
And all the ends of the earth shall fear Him [with awe-inspired reverence and submissive wonder].

MSG.1:
God, mark us with grace
and blessing! Smile!
MSG.2:
The whole country will see how you work,
all the godless nations see how you save.
MSG.3:
God! Let people thank and enjoy you.
Let all people thank and enjoy you.
MSG.4:
Let all far-flung people become happy
and shout their happiness because
You judge them fair and square,
you tend the far-flung peoples.
MSG.5:
God! Let people thank and enjoy you.
Let all people thank and enjoy you.
MSG.6:
Earth, display your exuberance!
You mark us with blessing, O God, our God.
MSG.7:
You mark us with blessing, O God.
Earth's four corners—honor him!

NIV.1:
Psalm 67
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. A psalm. A song.
May God be gracious to us and bless us
and make his face shine upon us, Selah
NIV.2:
that your ways may be known on earth,
your salvation among all nations.
NIV.3:
May the peoples praise you, O God;
may all the peoples praise you.
NIV.4:
May the nations be glad and sing for joy,
for you rule the peoples justly
and guide the nations of the earth. Selah
NIV.5:
May the peoples praise you, O God;
may all the peoples praise you.
NIV.6:
Then the land will yield its harvest,
and God, our God, will bless us.
NIV.7:
God will bless us,
and all the ends of the earth will fear him.

NRSVCE.1:
May God be gracious to us and bless us
and make his face to shine upon us, Selah
NRSVCE.2:
that your way may be known upon earth,
your saving power among all nations.
NRSVCE.3:
Let the peoples praise you, O God;
let all the peoples praise you.
NRSVCE.4:
Let the nations be glad and sing for joy,
for you judge the peoples with equity
and guide the nations upon earth. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
Let the peoples praise you, O God;
let all the peoples praise you.
NRSVCE.6:
The earth has yielded its increase;
God, our God, has blessed us.
NRSVCE.7:
May God continue to bless us;
let all the ends of the earth revere him.

NET.1: May God show us his favor and bless us! May he smile on us! (Selah)
NET.2: Then those living on earth will know what you are like; all nations will know how you deliver your people.
NET.3: Let the nations thank you, O God! Let all the nations thank you!
NET.4: Let foreigners rejoice and celebrate! For you execute justice among the nations, and govern the people living on earth. (Selah)
NET.5: Let the nations thank you, O God! Let all the nations thank you!
NET.6: The earth yields its crops. May God, our God, bless us!
NET.7: May God bless us! Then all the ends of the earth will give him the honor he deserves.
